ourStory.meta.title: Our story
ourStory.meta.description: The story behind AeroX, the virtual wind tunnel for cyclists and triathletes. Optimize your aero on a smart trainer, ride faster, save watts.
ourStory.hero.title: AeroX: from pain point to breakthrough
ourStory.hero.subtitle: A triathlon problem turned into a tool any cyclist can use
ourStory.p1: It started with long hours on a smart trainer. Power up, numbers up… then race day: climbing, I caught riders; on the flat, I got dropped. The reason? Aerodynamics — the invisible drag that’s ~70 % of resistance at 25 km/h and up to 95 % at high speed.
ourStory.p2: Like many, I thought higher Zwift watts meant real progress. I wasn’t training my aero position. Outside, I held it for minutes at best. I blamed bikes and wheels… until my CNRS background reminded me: ~80 % of aero losses come from the rider, not the gear. The real brake was me.
ourStory.p3: Feel can fool you. Pushing harder isn’t the answer. A raised head or wide aerobars can cost dozens of watts and several km/h. Indoors, nothing forces you to learn or correct your posture. Worse, Zwift tucks your avatar downhill when you stop pedaling — misleading. Result: zero feedback. Zero constraints. Zero lasting progress.
ourStory.p4: Out of that frustration came AeroX: an app that turns your smart trainer into a virtual wind tunnel. With just a webcam, you see in real time how position changes affect speed. You save watts and go faster — like a pro wind tunnel at €1800/day — but from your living room, for the price of a coffee a day.
ourStory.p5: From the first tests, it was clear: narrower aerobars, head tucked, position held — I gained 3 km/h in races. Beyond a personal tool, AeroX lets thousands of cyclists and triathletes learn aero, measure it, feel it, and finally train smart so every watt turns into speed.
ourStory.team.title: The team behind AeroX
ourStory.team.subtitle: Two builders, complementary paths, one mission: democratize aerodynamics
ourStory.team.olivier.desc: Former CNRS researcher and passionate triathlete, Olivier built AeroX to solve his own aero roadblocks. He now brings scientific rigor and athlete insight to riders — amateur to elite — who want to go faster.
ourStory.team.milan.desc: Elite triathlete, 2nd at the European Junior Cup (2025). Milan represents the new generation. His top-level feedback drives AeroX’s development so it fits real racing demands — even for the most demanding athletes.

home.meta.description: AeroX turns your smart trainer into a virtual wind tunnel. Measure your position with a webcam, gain 3 km/h and save 40 W. Try it now.
home.hero.subtitle: Measure your frontal area in real time, optimize your position and turn every watt into speed. Compatible with Wahoo, Elite, Tacx, Saris.
home.hero.paragraph:
Gain up to 3 km/h, save 40 W
 — with just a webcam.
home.hero.cta.seo: Download AeroX, an indoor-cycling app with aerodynamics
home.definition: AeroX is the reference application for indoor cycling aerodynamic analysis. Using just a webcam, AeroX measures the cyclist's frontal area and evaluates their CdA (Cx × A) in real time. An accessible alternative to wind tunnels and high-tech sensors, the application provides instant feedback on the aerodynamic impact of every position change, to train every day to optimize your speed on the bike.
home.compat.title: Compatibility
home.compat.subtitle: Works with major smart-trainer and HR brands for a smooth, reliable indoor setup. Tacx, Elite, Wahoo, Saris — you’re covered.

home.testimonials.title: Testimonials
home.testimonials.subtitle: Triathletes, cyclists and coaches — they chose AeroX
home.testimonials.subsubtitle: Amateur and elite triathletes, cyclists and coaches explain how they gain speed and save watts with real-time aerodynamics. A practical alternative to Zwift, MyWhoosh and Kinomap.
home.testimonials.items.milan.text: "Incredible that something so simple is this accurate. I save 35 W just with my position. I’ve got more energy for the run."
home.testimonials.items.frederic.text: "In 4 weeks, +1 km/h without forcing. I didn’t think aero mattered that much at my level. Now I enjoy riding more, even in the wind. Real innovation versus Zwift."
home.testimonials.items.olivier.text: "Former CNRS researcher and triathlete, I built AeroX for myself — it gave me +3 km/h in races. Now I want to democratize this feedback once reserved for pros. A simple webcam is enough."
home.testimonials.items.james.text:
"My athletes finally see the impact of their position. With Zwift they controlled power; now I have a real lever for free speed.
Thank you, AeroX."

home.features.title: How can you gain speed without pedaling harder?
home.features.subtitle: Every watt counts — and so does your budget.
home.features.ctaDetails: Only left to become an AeroX pioneer and help build the future of indoor cycling!
home.features.items.gain.title: Gain 1 to 5 km/h with no extra effort
home.features.items.gain.desc: See, in real time, how many km/h your position is costing you. +1 km/h at 25 km/h; up to +5 km/h at 45 km/h. Better than a €5000 bike.
home.features.items.gain.education:
From 25 km/h, air eats ~70 % of your power (up to 95 % at 45). Shrink frontal area, get speed back. Elbows in, head low, narrow bars — AeroX shows the gains as you move.

👉 Finish rides getting home faster without pushing harder — slicing through air. That’s performance and fun.
home.features.items.gain.aria: Download AeroX, indoor-cycling app to gain 1–5 km/h with your aerodynamics
home.features.items.real.title: Gain real speed, not virtual power
home.features.items.real.desc: On a trainer you progress in a virtual world. With AeroX, you save 40 W — and get faster outside.
home.features.items.real.education: Smart trainers push you to chase power — great for virtual fun. Outside, you’re not always faster, and aero may feel awkward. That’s 40 W lost. Real-time aero feedback both optimizes your position and turns it into a habit, so you enjoy outdoor rides and perform when it counts.
home.features.items.pro.title: Train like a pro — without an elite budget
home.features.items.pro.desc: Pros master power, heart rate… and aero. A wind tunnel? From €1500. AeroX brings the same lever to your smart trainer — with just a webcam.
home.features.items.pro.education:
Classic plans cover power and HR. But TT champions also hold power in optimal aero. Testing aero is out of reach for 99 % of riders (wind tunnels, velodromes, sensors). AeroX changes that: real-time position data from home training.

👉 What pros pay thousands for, you can now use at home — with that feeling: “I’m training like them.”
home.features.items.pro.cta: Train like a pro with AeroX
home.features.items.cost.title: Ride faster without spending thousands
home.features.items.cost.desc: 90 % of aero drag is your position — not bike, wheels or helmet. Learn to optimize it for less than aero socks.
home.features.items.cost.education: Deep wheels, high-end frames, fancy helmets… marketing sells a few watts for thousands of euros. In reality, your body creates most of the drag. The best bike won’t fix a position you can’t hold for 5 minutes. Invest where it pays: your position. Your top-end gear deserves top-end training.
home.features.items.proof.title: AeroX really works
home.features.items.proof.desc: I gained +3 km/h — from 36 to 39 km/h — on a 2008 €700 bike. No new bike needed.
home.features.items.proof.education: While building AeroX, I learned to hold aero from 25 km/h: narrower aerobars, longer posture, head and shoulders low. Even uphill, once speed passed 25, I stayed aero. Result: 36 → 39 km/h on a 2008 €700 bike, same FTP. I used to get dropped on the flat — not anymore. None of it without real-time feedback. — Olivier D., triathlete, AeroX founder

home.steps.title: Start training with AeroX in 2 minutes
home.steps.subsubtitle: Easy setup. Works instantly with your smart trainer. Compatible with major brands (Tacx, Garmin, Wahoo, Elite, Saris). As smooth as Zwift, MyWhoosh or Kinomap — with one unique advantage: real-time aero to gain speed and save watts.
home.steps.items.1.title: Step 1: Download AeroX
home.steps.items.2.title: Step 2: Place your camera (30 s)
home.steps.items.2.desc: Put your webcam ~1.3 m in front of the trainer. Check in AeroX that you’re fully visible on the bike — that’s it. Any webcam that connects to your computer works; even your phone over Wi-Fi.
home.steps.items.3.title: Step 3: Calibrate in 2 clicks (10 s)
home.steps.items.3.desc: Click both ends of your handlebar. First time, set bar width and camera distance. Adjust if needed — you’re ready. Settings are saved for next time.
home.steps.items.4.title: Step 4: Connect your smart trainer (20 s)
home.steps.items.4.desc: Bluetooth detection, no complex setup. AeroX finds your trainer and HR strap. Pick devices once; it auto-connects next time. Works with Wahoo, Elite, Tacx, Saris, Kickr, Suito, Zwift, etc. Garmin, Polar, Suunto for HR.
home.steps.items.5.title: Ride!
home.faq.title: Answers to your questions
home.faq.subtitle: Frequently asked questions
home.faq.items.1.title: Why AeroX?
home.faq.items.1.more: 80% of your effort fights the air — AeroX shows you where to get it back
home.faq.items.1.education: From 25 km/h, aerodynamics accounts for 70 to 95% of total resistance — it’s the number one factor slowing you down, far more than weight or equipment. Yet no standard smart trainer measures your aero position. On Zwift, MyWhoosh or Kinomap, you push harder without ever knowing if your position is costing you 30 or 50 wasted watts. AeroX changes the game: using a simple webcam, the app analyzes your silhouette in real time and calculates your frontal area (CdA). You instantly see how many km/h and watts each adjustment gains you. Results measured across 300+ sessions: +2 to 3 km/h of speed and 30 to 40 W saved at the same effort. That’s the equivalent of 30 to 45 minutes gained on an Ironman — without pedaling harder, just by optimizing your position.
home.faq.items.2.title: What gear do I need?
home.faq.items.2.education: To use AeroX, you need a computer (macOS or Windows), your bike on a support, and a camera — your laptop's built-in webcam, a USB webcam, or even your smartphone over Wi-Fi. That's all you need for basic aero analysis: AeroX measures your frontal area (CdA) in real time with no additional sensors. For Power Mode (position/watts link) and Speed Mode (resistance linked to your position), a Bluetooth FTMS smart trainer is required: Wahoo Kickr, Elite Direto, Tacx Neo, Saris H3 and most recent models are compatible. Heart rate monitors from Garmin, Polar and Suunto are also supported. Setup takes 2 minutes: place the camera about 1.3 m in front of the trainer, launch AeroX, calibrate in two clicks — and you're ready to ride.
home.faq.items.2.more: A computer, your smart trainer, a camera
home.faq.items.3.title: How long does setup take?
home.faq.items.3.education: Under 2 minutes. Place your webcam about 1.3 m in front of your trainer — on a tripod, a shelf, or even on a chair. Launch AeroX on your computer, click both ends of your handlebar to calibrate distance and width: done. The first time, you also set the camera-to-bike distance. These settings are saved: next sessions, you just launch and ride. AeroX automatically detects your smart trainer and heart rate strap via Bluetooth — no complex configuration. If your trainer isn’t interactive, you can still use visual analysis mode to see your frontal area change in real time. For comparison, a wind tunnel session requires 2 to 4 hours of travel and setup. With AeroX, you go from setup to pedaling in 120 seconds.
home.faq.items.3.more: In under 2 minutes, your trainer becomes a virtual wind tunnel…
home.faq.items.4.title: Is my trainer or sensor compatible with AeroX?
home.faq.items.4.education: AeroX is compatible with 95% of smart trainers on the market via Bluetooth FTMS: Wahoo Kickr, Elite Direto and Suito, Tacx Neo and Flux, Saris H3, and most recent models. For basic aero analysis (real-time CdA measurement), a smart trainer isn’t mandatory — a simple webcam and your bike on a support are enough. Power Mode and Speed Mode require an FTMS trainer to link resistance to your position. Heart rate monitors from Garmin, Polar and Suunto are supported. AeroX runs on macOS and Windows — no tablet or smartphone needed for the main screen. If your trainer doesn’t appear in the list, contact us: we regularly add new models. In short, if you already ride on Zwift or MyWhoosh, your equipment is compatible with AeroX.
home.faq.items.4.more: Compatible with 95% of smart trainers — probably yours…
home.faq.items.5.title: Innovation or gadget?
home.faq.items.5.more: Neither — AeroX is a paradigm shift in training: your speed becomes your priority
home.faq.items.5.education: 20 years ago, the heart rate monitor was a gadget. Today, no one trains without one. 10 years ago, the power meter was reserved for pros. Today, most serious cyclists use one. Aerodynamics is the next lever to democratize — and that's exactly what AeroX does. Built by Olivier Demichel, former CNRS researcher and triathlete, AeroX uses AI-powered video analysis to measure your frontal area in real time. This isn't a theoretical estimate: it's a physical measurement of your silhouette, frame by frame, with 3% repeatability. The gains are concrete and measured: +2 to 3 km/h on average across 300+ sessions. AeroX doesn't replace your power meter — it complements it by adding the aerodynamic dimension that no one was measuring on a smart trainer until now.
home.faq.items.6.title: I ride in a bunch — is this still useful?
home.faq.items.6.education: Cycling isn’t just sitting in the bunch. Echelons, breakaways, pulling — aero makes the difference. With +3 km/h, you might enjoy attacking.
home.faq.items.6.more: Yes — unless you plan to stay at the back!
home.faq.items.7.title: Do I need a subscription to find and test my optimal position?
home.faq.items.7.education: You can try AeroX from day one with the free plan: analyze your position, measure your CdA, see the impact of each adjustment in real time. That's enough to discover your main aero levers and see the possible gains. The subscription unlocks advanced features: Power Mode to link your position to your watts, Speed Mode where trainer resistance adapts to your aerodynamics, GPX simulation to prepare a real course, and time trials with rankings. Above all, the subscription turns a one-time discovery into a lasting habit. Holding an efficient aero position doesn't happen by chance — it's a technical skill that requires repetition, like pedaling out of the saddle or accelerating out of a corner. With regular training on AeroX, your aero position becomes a natural reflex: your sixth sense when you ride outdoors.
home.faq.items.7.more: Test with the free plan, progress with a subscription
home.faq.items.8.title: I’m already used to an aero position — is this still useful?
home.faq.items.8.more: Being used to it doesn’t mean being optimized — AeroX shows you the difference
home.faq.items.8.education: Being in position isn't enough — it also needs to be effective. Most cyclists think they're aerodynamic while invisible details cost them 10 to 30 watts: head 3 cm too high, elbows 5 cm too wide, shoulders not tucked in. Without objective measurement, you can't know. AeroX makes these details visible in real time: you adjust your head, your CdA drops 2% — you see it instantly on screen. Our measurements show that even experienced cyclists still gain 1 to 2 km/h by refining their position with objective feedback. Moreover, your position degrades with fatigue: AeroX shows you how your CdA evolves throughout the session, so you learn to hold an efficient position even after 2 hours of effort. It's the difference between thinking you're aero and measuring it.
home.faq.schema.a1: From 25 km/h, aerodynamics accounts for 70 to 95% of resistance. AeroX lets you visualize and optimize your position to ride faster and save watts.
home.faq.schema.q2: What tools and equipment do I need to use AeroX?
home.faq.schema.a2: All you need is a smart trainer (Bluetooth FTMS), your bike, a Mac or Windows computer, and a simple camera (webcam, smartphone, or USB).
home.faq.schema.q3: How long does it take to set up AeroX?
home.faq.schema.a3: Place your camera in front of your trainer, calibrate it in 2 clicks, and you're ready to train with real-time aero feedback. Less than 2 minutes is all it takes.
home.faq.schema.q4: Is my trainer or sensor compatible with AeroX?
home.faq.schema.a4: Yes, AeroX is compatible with most smart trainers (Wahoo, Elite, Tacx, Saris, etc.). Currently available on macOS and Windows, with tablet versions in development.
home.faq.schema.a5: Developed by a CNRS researcher and triathlete, AeroX provides concrete feedback on your aerodynamics. Like the heart rate monitor 20 years ago, AeroX is becoming an essential training tool.
home.faq.schema.q6: Is aerodynamics with AeroX useful when riding in a pack?
home.faq.schema.a6: Yes, because echelons, breakaways, and surges happen frequently. Gaining 3 km/h can make the difference between staying in the group or riding off the front.
home.faq.schema.q7: Do I need a subscription to try AeroX?
home.faq.schema.a7: You can try AeroX for free from day one. With a subscription, you turn your optimal position into a solid, lasting habit.
home.faq.schema.q8: I'm already used to my aero position — is AeroX still useful?
home.faq.schema.a8: Yes. Being in position isn't enough — it also needs to be efficient. AeroX detects invisible details (head too high, elbows too wide) and helps you correct those watt losses.
home.faq.schema.q9: Is a coach enough, or is AeroX complementary?
home.faq.schema.a9: Your coach can plan sessions, but only AeroX measures the real effectiveness of your position from start to finish. The two are complementary.
home.faq.schema.q10: How does AeroX help me prepare for a race abroad?
home.faq.schema.a10: With GPX mode, you import the exact course of your race. AeroX simulates the terrain, wind, and impact of your aerodynamics so you can train as if you were already there.

bf.meta.title: Bike Fitters — Measure Your Clients' Aerodynamics
bf.meta.description: Add real-time aerodynamic measurement to your bike fitting sessions. AeroX turns every adjustment into measurable speed gains. Request a demo.
bf.definition: Aerodynamic bike fitting optimizes a cyclist's position to reduce aerodynamic drag (CdA). Unlike traditional fitting focused on comfort and biomechanics, aero fitting measures how each adjustment — saddle height, aerobar angle, head position — affects the rider's frontal area. With AeroX, this measurement happens in real time using a webcam and a smart trainer, right in the bike fitter's studio. The client instantly sees how many watts and km/h each change is worth. The result: a more convincing session, a loyal client, and a differentiating service that few fitters offer today.
bf.hero.text: You know your fitting makes a difference. Your clients don't see it yet. AeroX makes every adjustment measurable in real time.
bf.benefits.title: Why add aero measurement to your bike fitting studio?
bf.benefits.b1.title: Prove your results
bf.benefits.b1.desc: Show your clients, live, how many km/h and watts each adjustment gains them. No more "trust me" — let the numbers speak.
bf.benefits.b2.title: Differentiate your offer
bf.benefits.b2.desc: Offer aerodynamic fitting that nobody else provides in your area. A webcam is all you need — no wind tunnel required.
bf.benefits.b3.title: Retain your clients
bf.benefits.b3.desc: A client who sees their gains comes back. AeroX creates objective follow-up: every session shows real progression.
bf.benefits.b4.title: Simple integration

bf.metrics.title: What does AeroX measure during a bike fitting session?
bf.metrics.intro: In a traditional fitting, you adjust the saddle, handlebars, and cleats. With AeroX, every adjustment instantly translates into measurable data your client sees live.
bf.metrics.m1.title: CdA — aerodynamic frontal area
bf.metrics.m1.desc: Measure your client's drag coefficient in real time. Compare two positions and show the difference in numbers.
bf.metrics.m2.title: Watts saved
bf.metrics.m2.desc: AeroX calculates watts gained between two positions at the same speed. An aerobar adjustment can save 15 to 40 W.
bf.metrics.m3.title: Time saved per kilometer
bf.metrics.m3.desc: Translate watts into seconds saved per km. Over an Ironman, a few saved watts = 30 to 45 minutes gained on the bike.
bf.metrics.m4.title: Estimated speed at your client's level
bf.metrics.m4.desc: AeroX adapts projections to your client's actual FTP. A CdA gain doesn't have the same impact at 180 W vs 280 W.
bf.metrics.outro: In short: AeroX turns your fitting into a measurable and memorable experience. The client leaves with concrete numbers, not just a feeling.
bf.links.blog: Read: Bike Fitting Toward Performance Optimization
bf.steps.title: How it works in 3 steps
bf.steps.s1.title: Set up in 2 minutes
bf.steps.s1.desc: Place a webcam facing the smart trainer in your studio. Launch AeroX on your computer. That's it — no complex calibration, no extra sensors.
bf.steps.s2.title: Fit as usual
bf.steps.s2.desc: Adjust saddle, handlebars, aerobars. AeroX continuously analyzes the silhouette and displays CdA, watts saved, and estimated speed in real time on your screen.
bf.steps.s3.title: Show the results
bf.steps.s3.desc: At the end of the session, your client sees it in black and white: −35 W, +2.8 km/h, 42 min saved over an Ironman. They leave convinced — and they come back.
bf.faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
bf.faq.q1: How much does AeroX cost for a bike fitter?
bf.faq.short1: Two pro licenses (Standard and Premium), starting from €109/month. Immediate ROI.
bf.faq.more1: License details and wind tunnel comparison
bf.faq.a1: We offer two professional licenses adapted to bike fitters' needs, available monthly or annually. The Fit AeroX Standard version provides dynamic aerodynamic analysis without power data: ideal for visualizing the impact of each adjustment on frontal area. The Premium version adds position/power analysis and Speed Mode, where the smart trainer's resistance is linked to position — the client physically feels the aerodynamic impact of each change. For comparison, a wind tunnel session costs $1,500 to $3,000 and requires travel to a specialized facility. With AeroX, measurement is integrated into your studio with no expensive additional hardware. The ROI is immediate: a client who sees their gains comes back, recommends your studio, and accepts a higher rate for a differentiating service. Contact us for more information and a quote tailored to your practice.
bf.faq.short2: A webcam and your existing setup. No additional hardware required.
bf.faq.more2: Standard vs Premium: which equipment for which license
bf.faq.a2: For the Standard version (aero analysis without power): a webcam (built-in or external, 720p minimum) and your client's bike on a support — the trainer doesn't need to be a smart trainer, a static analysis is enough. For the Premium version (position/power analysis and Speed Mode): a Bluetooth FTMS-compatible smart trainer is required (Wahoo Kickr, Elite Direto, Tacx Neo, Saris H3…). Setup takes 2 minutes: place the webcam about 1.3 m in front of the trainer, launch AeroX, and calibrate in two clicks. Your laptop's built-in webcam works fine; you can also use a smartphone over Wi-Fi as a camera. Heart rate monitors from Garmin, Polar, and Suunto are also supported. In short: no specialized hardware investment required, just the equipment you already have in your studio.
bf.faq.q3: Do I need training to use AeroX?
bf.faq.short3: 30-min onboarding included — training in aerodynamics applied to bike fitting.
bf.faq.more3: What the onboarding covers and why it changes your practice
bf.faq.a3: A personalized 30-minute onboarding is included for every new partner. It covers installation, calibration, and feature discovery — but above all, it trains you on aerodynamics applied to bike fitting. The goal: give you the keys to explain to your clients why aero costs time on the clock, especially for slower riders where every saved watt matters even more. After this onboarding, you're autonomous. The interface was designed to be used live during the session, without interrupting your workflow. Data is displayed in real time: CdA, watts saved, estimated speed — your client sees it while you adjust. Priority support is included. Our current partners — from Brazil to the United States to Europe — offer aero analysis to all their clients from their very first session.
bf.faq.q4: Can my clients continue using AeroX at home?
bf.faq.short4: Yes, with bidirectional data sharing between you and your clients.
bf.faq.more4: A retention lever for your business
bf.faq.a4: Yes, and it's a real retention lever for your business. You can recommend AeroX to your clients so they maintain and work on their position between sessions on their own smart trainer. They progress, see real-time results, and naturally come back for follow-up sessions. On their AeroX account, the client can access their fitting data and track their progress. On your side, as a bike fitter, you also have access to your clients' data: you can follow their evolution between appointments and identify areas to refine in the next session. AeroX runs on macOS and Windows, with the same webcam and smart trainer as in your studio. The client finds a familiar environment that extends your expertise and creates a lasting connection between your sessions.
bf.faq.q5: How accurate is the aerodynamic measurement?
bf.faq.short5: 10% absolute, 3% repeatability — ideal for comparing positions live.
bf.faq.more5: Why repeatability matters more than absolute accuracy in fitting
bf.faq.a5: AeroX measures aerodynamic frontal area (CdA) in real time via AI-powered video analysis. The algorithm analyzes the cyclist's silhouette frame by frame to calculate the projected area facing the wind. Absolute accuracy is 10%, with 3% measurement repeatability — less precise than a wind tunnel in absolute terms, but far more versatile. AeroX provides real-time information anywhere and enables effective comparison of position and fit changes. You adjust the saddle by 5 mm, the CdA updates live. That's exactly what a bike fitter needs in a session: not an absolute lab value, but an instant, visual comparison between each tested position. In a professional wind tunnel, a measurement costs $1,500 to $3,000 and gives a one-time result. AeroX provides continuous measurement, directly usable during fitting, accessible from your studio.

cda.meta.title: CdA in cycling: definition, values and performance impact
cda.meta.description: CdA (Cx × A) is the key metric of cycling aerodynamics. Learn its physical definition, typical values by category and how to measure it with AeroX.
cda.intro: CdA is the most important metric for quantifying a cyclist's aerodynamic efficiency. Understanding CdA explains why some riders are faster than others at the same power output.
cda.definition.p1: CdA stands for Coefficient of Drag × Area. It is the product of two quantities:
cda.definition.cx: Cx (or Cd): the drag coefficient, a dimensionless number that depends on body shape and airflow. A more streamlined profile has a lower Cx.
cda.definition.a: A: the frontal area, measured in square meters (m²), representing the cross-sectional area the cyclist presents to the airflow.
cda.definition.formula: CdA = Cx × A, expressed in m².
cda.definition.p2: The power required to overcome aerodynamic drag follows the equation: Paero = 0.5 × ρ × CdA × v³, where ρ is air density (~1.225 kg/m³ at sea level) and v is velocity. Aero power scales with the cube of speed: doubling your speed requires 8× more watts to fight the air. Conversely, even a small CdA reduction frees up watts at every speed.
cda.why.h2: Why does CdA matter so much?
cda.why.p1: Above 25 km/h, aerodynamic drag accounts for over 80% of the total resistance a cyclist fights. In a time trial at 45 km/h, this reaches 90-95%. Reducing CdA is therefore the most powerful performance lever — far more impactful than adding watts.
cda.why.p2: A cyclist who goes from an upright position (frontal area ~0.50 m²) to an optimized compact position (~0.42 m²) can gain 2-3 km/h at the same power. With aerobars (~0.33 m²), gains reach 4-5 km/h. The bigger the reduction, the bigger the gain — and body position offers far larger gains than equipment.
cda.values.city.label: City bike, upright position
cda.values.road_hoods.label: Road bike, hands on hoods
cda.values.road_drops.label: Road bike, hands in drops
cda.values.tt.label: Time trial position
cda.values.pro_tt.label: Professional TT, optimized
cda.values.track.label: Track pursuit (skin suit + aero helmet)
cda.impact.p1: The impact depends on your speed and the extent of the position change. Concrete measured examples:
cda.impact.item1: Compact road position (pos 1→4, -20% frontal area): ~33 W saved at 30 km/h, ~53 W at 35 km/h
cda.impact.item2: Optimized aerobars (pos 1→6, -37% frontal area): ~63 W saved at 30 km/h, ~101 W at 35 km/h
cda.impact.item3: Time gain: at 25 km/h, an optimized position saves ~10 s/km. At 35 km/h, ~7 s/km. The slower you ride, the bigger the time gain per kilometer
cda.impact.item4: On an Ironman (180 km) at 30 km/h: a compact position (pos 4) saves ~24 min. With optimized aerobars (pos 6), the gain reaches ~50 min
cda.measure.methods.tunnel: Wind tunnel: the gold standard, but expensive (€500–2,000/session) and limited access. Repeatability: 2-3%.
cda.measure.methods.velodrome: Velodrome testing: requires a track and power meter. Repeatability: 5-8%. Cost: €200-500.
cda.measure.methods.chung: Chung method (outdoor): free with a power meter, but wind and road dependent. Repeatability: 10-15%.
cda.measure.methods.aerox: AeroX (virtual wind tunnel): uses a webcam and smart trainer to measure CdA in real time. Repeatability: 3%, accessible from home.
cda.measure.aerox.p1: AeroX is the first application that turns a smart trainer into a virtual wind tunnel. Using AI and computer vision, AeroX analyzes the cyclist's silhouette in real time via a standard webcam and calculates CdA instantly.
cda.measure.aerox.p2: The cyclist can test different positions (handlebar height, aerobar width, torso angle, head position) and see the CdA impact immediately. This real-time feedback loop enables rapid and effective optimization.
cda.optimize.item1: Body position (80% of drag): torso angle, head position, arm spacing. The rider's body accounts for the vast majority of aerodynamic drag.
cda.optimize.item2: Equipment: aero helmet (-5 to 10 W), fitted skinsuit (-3 to 8 W), shoe covers (-1 to 3 W).
cda.optimize.item3: Bike: aero frame, deep-section wheels, aerobars. Real impact but secondary to body position.
cda.optimize.item4: Accessories: bottles, bags, race numbers — every exposed item adds drag.
cda.conclusion.p1: Most cyclists invest in power (training, nutrition) and equipment (frame, wheels) but overlook CdA — the single most impactful factor on speed above 25 km/h. Measuring and optimizing CdA is now accessible to everyone with tools like AeroX.
cda.lastUpdated: Last updated: March 2026. Data based on 300+ AeroX sessions and published cycling aerodynamics research.
